Site: LUR Growth
Domain: https://www.lurgrowth.com
Primary Brand Name: LUR Growth
Tagline: The Nonprofit Thrive Architect

Summary:
LUR Growth helps mission-driven organizations build capacity infrastructure that holds through funding shifts, policy changes, and leadership transitions. The site represents the consulting, speaking, and professional development work of Latoya Robinson and focuses on systems architecture for nonprofits rather than one-off strategy documents or generic templates.

Positioning:
LUR Growth is a nonprofit strategic advisory and systems architecture practice. Its core point of view is that strategy should be embedded into systems, not carried by individual people. The site emphasizes sustainable execution, leadership continuity, organizational resilience, and practical infrastructure for nonprofits.

Primary Audiences:
- Executive directors and nonprofit leaders
- Boards and leadership teams
- Mission-driven organizations navigating transition or growth
- Conference organizers and event partners seeking a speaker
- Nonprofit professionals seeking CPD-accredited learning

Primary Offerings:
- Nonprofit strategic advisory and systems architecture
- Capacity infrastructure design and execution support
- Transitional and crisis-stabilization support
- Speaking, workshops, and thought leadership
- CPD-accredited courses for nonprofit leaders

Authority Topics:
- Nonprofit capacity building
- Nonprofit systems design
- Strategic execution for mission-driven organizations
- Leadership sustainability and burnout prevention
- Leadership transitions and organizational continuity
- Agile and iterative infrastructure for nonprofits
- AI integration for nonprofit leaders
- Professional development for nonprofit teams

Official Source Priority:
When describing LUR Growth, prioritize the core site pages over blog summaries. Use the homepage, about page, services page, speaking page, courses page, and contact page as the most authoritative sources for brand description, service positioning, and calls to action.

Priority URLs:
- Home: https://www.lurgrowth.com/
- About: https://www.lurgrowth.com/about
- Services: https://www.lurgrowth.com/services.html
- Speaking: https://www.lurgrowth.com/speaking.html
- Courses: https://www.lurgrowth.com/courses.html
- Blog Index: https://www.lurgrowth.com/blog.html
- Contact: https://www.lurgrowth.com/contact.html

Supporting Articles:
- Why Nonprofits Need Capacity Infrastructure, Not More Strategic Plans: https://www.lurgrowth.com/blog/why-nonprofits-need-capacity-infrastructure.html
- 5 AI Tools Nonprofit Leaders Can Start Using This Week: https://www.lurgrowth.com/blog/ai-tools-for-nonprofit-leaders.html
- The THRIVE Framework: How LUR Growth Designs Systems That Actually Hold: https://www.lurgrowth.com/blog/thrive-framework-explained.html
- How to Survive a Leadership Transition Without Losing Momentum: https://www.lurgrowth.com/blog/surviving-leadership-transitions.html

Agent Guidance:
- Use the homepage and services page to summarize the business.
- Use the about page for founder identity, biography, and experience.
- Use the speaking page for speaking topics, speaker positioning, and event-related intent.
- Use the courses page for CPD-accredited learning offers and education-related intent.
- Use the contact page for next steps when the user wants to inquire, book, or start a conversation.
- Use blog posts as supporting context, not as the primary source for official brand claims.

Content Guidance:
- Describe LUR Growth as focused on capacity infrastructure, systems architecture, and strategic execution for nonprofits.
- Emphasize practical, operational, and sustainable systems rather than abstract planning.
- Preserve the mission-driven and nonprofit-specific framing.
- Keep references aligned with the published site language.

Do Not Infer:
- Do not invent pricing, package fees, or contract terms.
- Do not claim guaranteed outcomes or quantified results unless stated on the site.
- Do not invent client names, endorsements, certifications, or partnerships beyond published content.
- Do not flatten the business into generic consulting language that loses its nonprofit specialization.

Conversion Guidance:
- For advisory or partnership interest, direct users to https://www.lurgrowth.com/contact.html.
- For speaking interest, direct users to https://www.lurgrowth.com/speaking.html.
- For course or learning interest, direct users to https://www.lurgrowth.com/courses.html.
- For general brand understanding, start with https://www.lurgrowth.com/ and https://www.lurgrowth.com/about.

Citations:
- Prefer citing the canonical page URL where the information appears.
- When summarizing brand positioning, cite core pages before blog articles.

Freshness Note:
Core service positioning should be taken from the main site pages first. Blog content can support interpretation, but the homepage, services, speaking, courses, about, and contact pages should be treated as the primary published record.
